selectHint: Select a status code to view details

100.desc: The server has received the request headers and the client should proceed to send the request body.

101.desc: The server is switching protocols as requested by the client.

103.desc: Used to return some response headers before final HTTP message.

201.desc: The request has been fulfilled and a new resource has been created.

202.desc: The request has been accepted for processing, but the processing has not been completed.

204.desc: The server successfully processed the request and is not returning any content.

206.desc: The server is delivering only part of the resource due to a range header sent by the client.

301.desc: The requested resource has been permanently moved to a new URL.

302.desc: The requested resource resides temporarily under a different URL.

304.desc: The resource has not been modified since the version specified by the request headers.

307.desc: The requested resource resides temporarily under a different URL, method must not change.

308.desc: The requested resource has been permanently moved, method must not change.

400.desc: The server cannot or will not process the request due to a client error.

401.desc: Authentication is required and has failed or has not been provided.

403.desc: The server understood the request but refuses to authorize it.

404.desc: The requested resource could not be found on the server.

405.desc: The request method is not supported for the requested resource.

408.desc: The server timed out waiting for the request.

409.desc: The request conflicts with the current state of the server.

410.desc: The resource requested is no longer available and will not be available again.

422.desc: The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic errors.

429.desc: The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time.

500.desc: The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.

501.desc: The server does not support the functionality required to fulfill the request.

502.desc: The server received an invalid response from the upstream server.

503.desc: The server is currently unable to handle the request due to maintenance or overloading.

504.desc: The server did not receive a timely response from the upstream server.

507.desc: The server is unable to store the representation needed to complete the request.
